name: Tortilla Soup-2
recipeIngredient: 2 Cans Jalapeno Pinto Beans, (e.g., Ranch Style brand)
5 Tablespoons "Mexene" Chili Seasoning, (similar to chili powder)
Crisp Tortilla Chips and Grated Cheese -- for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, boil chicken with onion until tender (10 minutes). Remove the chicken and reserve the stock.
2. Add beans and tomatoes to the pot (do not drain).
3. In a measuring cup, mix dry ingredients with just enough water to make a paste, ensuring all lumps are stirred out. Pour this mixture into the pot.
4. Add 2 cans of reserved chicken stock to the pot.
5. Float jalapeno slices and garlic buttons in the soup.
6. Bring the soup to a boil for 30 minutes, then reduce heat and simmer for an additional hour.
7. While waiting for the soup to finish cooking, shred the chicken into bite-sized pieces and add it back to the pot.
8. (The soup actually tastes better the next day; you may want to prepare it ahead of time.)
9. Just before serving, cut tortillas in half, then cut the halves into 1-inch strips. Add these to the simmering pot.
10. Spoon the soup into bowls and garnish with a few crisp tortilla chips and grated cheddar cheese. A dollop of sour cream and a few cilantro leaves are also nice additions.
